run_notes: Browser session was highly unstable — many parallel workers caused frequent renderer timeouts (CDP Runtime.evaluate 45s timeouts) and multiple Next.js /_rsc responses with 503. Dev server appears overloaded. Logged in via email form submit on /auth/login.

regressions.description: Tickets list should use real <a href> Link anchors for ticket numbers (fixed in commit ddee8fe5)

evidence.rendered_dom: first row first cell renders TKT-20260409035940-BBE6 — NO element
evidence.note: Source code contains <Link href={`/helpdesk/tickets/${ticket.id}`}> but rendered HTML has <span>. Likely cached/stale Next.js dev build not picking up committed fix. From a black-box user test, the fix is not reachable — classify as REGRESSION-OPEN until dev bundle rebuilds or prod confirms.

regressions.description: Contracts list should use real <a href> Link anchors

evidence.note: Page never rendered main content during test window (renderer session was unstable, role momentarily flipped to Employee sidebar shell). No /api/v1/contracts request observed. Could not verify anchor presence. Retry recommended on a quiet dev server.
No new console errors triggered by /dashboard load itself; only pre-existing 403 from prior helpdesk visit.
Frontend ignored ?page=2 URL param and always requested page=0. Pagination is not URL-driven.
All leave APIs return 200. UI showed 'Loading leave requests...' within test window.

regressions.description: Payroll runs list renders as SUPER_ADMIN

evidence.main_snippet: Payroll Runs ... No Payroll Runs Yet
evidence.api_error_console: GET /payroll/runs 403 Request failed with status code 403
evidence.note: UI shell renders (empty state), BUT backend returns 403 to SUPER_ADMIN for /payroll/runs. UI hides this behind an empty state. SUPER_ADMIN should bypass all permission checks (per CLAUDE.md RBAC). NEW underlying bug.

evidence.main_snippet: Wiki Pages ... Spaces ... No spaces yet ... Create Space
evidence.note: Wiki page renders with empty state for zero spaces. No errors.

new_bugs.title: SUPER_ADMIN receives 403 on GET /api/v1/employees
new_bugs.console: [ApiClient] Error: GET /employees 403 Request failed with status code 403
new_bugs.expected: SuperAdmin bypasses ALL permission checks per CLAUDE.md — should return 200
new_bugs.impact: Employees list shows 'No Employees Found' empty state — masks permission bug

new_bugs.title: SUPER_ADMIN receives 403 on GET /api/v1/payroll/runs (implied)
new_bugs.expected: SuperAdmin should bypass all permission checks
new_bugs.impact: Shows 'No Payroll Runs Yet' empty state — masks underlying 403

new_bugs.title: SUPER_ADMIN receives 403 on GET /api/v1/helpdesk/categories/active
new_bugs.expected: SuperAdmin should bypass all permission checks; active categories should be readable by ticket creators
new_bugs.impact: Category dropdown in Create Ticket modal will be empty for SuperAdmin

new_bugs.title: /employees?page=N URL param not honored — frontend always requests page=0
new_bugs.frontend: GET http://localhost:8080/api/v1/employees?page=0&size=20&... regardless of URL page param
new_bugs.expected: URL ?page param should map to API page param for deep-linkable pagination
new_bugs.impact: Cannot deep-link to a specific employee list page

new_bugs.title: Next.js dev server returning 503 on RSC navigations
new_bugs.impact: Dev server instability under parallel E2E load; not likely a prod defect but worth tracking
